items.title: Tim Cook to step down as Apple chief executive
items.standfirst: Apple has announced the chief executive who took over from Steve Jobs is to step down and has named his successor.
items.content: Apple has announced the chief executive who took over from Steve Jobs is to step down and has named his successor.

items.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook to step down after 15 years
items.standfirst: Cook, who took over from the late Steve Jobs in 2011, will remain involved with Apple.
items.content: Apple has announced its biggest leadership change in 15 years, with Tim Cook set to hand over the role of Chief Executive to hardware boss John Ternus on September 1, 2026. Cook, who took over from the late Steve Jobs in 2011, will remain involved in the company as Executive Chairman, focused on policy engagement and board-level responsibilities. Ternus, 50, has spent almost his entire career at Apple, joining in 2001 and most recently serving as Senior Vice President of Hardware Engineering.LIVE UPDATES: Trump says he's 'highly unlikely' to renew ceasefireHe led the teams behind the iPad, AirPods, and the latest iPhone lineup, including the newly released iPhone Air. Under Cook's leadership, Apple grew its market capitalisation from roughly $350 billion to $4 trillion — a more than tenfold increase. The company now operates in more than 200 countries, and its products are used by more than 2.5 billion people worldwide. "It has been the greatest privilege of my life to be the CEO of Apple and to have been trusted to lead such an extraordinary company," Cook said in a statement. "I love Apple with all of my being, and I am so grateful to have had the opportunity to work with a team of such ingenious, innovative, creative, and deeply caring people who have been unwavering in their dedication to enriching the lives of our customers and creating the best products and services in the world.READ MORE: Inside 'kitty flipping', the recreational drug trend booming in Aussie cities"John Ternus has the mind of an engineer, the soul of an innovator, and the heart to lead with integrity and with honour. He is a visionary whose contributions to Apple over 25 years are already too numerous to count, and he is without question the right person to lead Apple into the future. "I could not be more confident in his abilities and his character, and I look forward to working closely with him on this transition and in my new role as executive chairman." The transition was approved unanimously by Apple's board of directors. Arthur Levinson, Apple's non-executive chairman for the past 15 years, will move to the role of Lead Independent Director. Ternus will join the board when he becomes CEO.NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here

items.title: Queensland man killed in workplace accident involving forklift
items.standfirst: Emergency services were called to Karreman Quarries on West Mount Cotton Road in Sheldon about 2.20pm yesterday following reports a man had been critically injured in a forklift incident.
items.content: A worker has been killed in a horrific workplace accident involving a forklift in south-east Queensland.Emergency services were called to Karreman Quarries on West Mount Cotton Road in Sheldon, located around 30 kilometres from Brisbane, about 2.20pm yesterday following reports a man had been critically injured in a forklift incident.The man died from his injuries, a spokesperson for Resources, Safety and Health Queensland (RSHQ) confirmed today.EXCLUSIVE: 'Demoralising': Jacques has faced over 100 job rejections after redundancyREAD MORE: Bodyguard allegedly snatched from home and assaulted in botched abduction "Our thoughts are with the worker's family, friends and colleagues at this time," the spokesperson said."Enquiries into the incident are under way."As this is an active investigation no further comment can be made at this time."NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here

clusters.title: Bodyguard allegedly snatched from home in botched Sydney abduction
clusters.neutral_headline: Bodyguard allegedly snatched from home in botched Sydney abduction

items.title: Bodyguard allegedly snatched from home in botched Sydney abduction
items.standfirst: The victim – believed to be the bodyguard of a figure who is understood to have links to the Alameddine crime network in Sydney – was forced into his own car outside his home in Guildford.
items.content: Five men have been arrested after a man was allegedly abducted from his home, bundled into a car and assaulted in western Sydney overnight.Police swooped on the group of alleged kidnappers about 11.30pm after the victim – believed to be the bodyguard of a figure, who is understood to have links to the Alameddine crime network in Sydney – was forced into his own car outside his home in Guildford.The Ford Ranger was found partially burnt-out in Villawood a short time later.LIVE UPDATES: Trump may have let slip the real reason for his war READ MORE: 'Kitty flipping' sounds cute. It's a killer invading Australia An hour later at 12.30am, police tracked the victim to Casula in the city's south-west. He was treated for facial injuries and was taken to hospital in a stable condition.A group of five men were then arrested near the Hume Highway in Casula with the assistance of the Raptor Squad.Footage obtained by 9News shows police, including members of the Tactical Operations Unit, shows officers searching a nearby safehouse.MONEY: Millions of Australian workers to get instant $1000 tax deductionThe arrested men have been taken to Liverpool Station.No charges have been laid.A crime scene has been established and an investigation into the alleged abduction and assault is under way. NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here

clusters.title: US singer D4vd pleads not guilty to murdering 14yo girl found in his Tesla
clusters.neutral_headline: US singer D4vd pleads not guilty to murdering 14yo girl found in his Tesla
bullet_summary: The 21-year-old, who rose to fame after going viral on TikTok, has pleaded not guilty to a murder charge of a 14-year-old whose dismembered body was found in his seemingly abandoned Tesla
The long-dead body of Celeste Rivas Hernandez, 14, was found inside a Tesla that was towed from the Hollywood Hills on September 8, one day after she would have turned 15

items.title: US singer D4vd pleads not guilty to murdering 14yo girl found in his Tesla
items.standfirst: The 21-year-old, who rose to fame after going viral on TikTok, has pleaded not guilty to a murder charge of a 14-year-old whose dismembered body was found in his seemingly abandoned Tesla.
items.content: The 21-year-old, who rose to fame after going viral on TikTok, has pleaded not guilty to a murder charge of a 14-year-old whose dismembered body was found in his seemingly abandoned Tesla.

items.title: Singer D4vd charged with murdering teen found dismembered in his car
items.standfirst: The long-dead body of Celeste Rivas Hernandez, 14, was found inside a Tesla that was towed from the Hollywood Hills on September 8, one day after she would have turned 15.
items.content: Singer D4vd has been charged with murder over the death of a 14-year-old girl whose dismembered and decomposed body was found in September in his apparently abandoned Tesla, prosecutors say.The Los Angeles County District Attorney's Office on Monday (early Tuesday AEST) said the 21-year-old D4vd, whose legal name is David Burke, was charged with first-degree murder; lewd and lascivious acts with an individual under 14; and mutilating a body over the killing of Celeste Rivas Hernandez.She was reported missing by her family in 2024, when she was 13. Authorities say she was 14 when she died.READ MORE: Dezi Freeman's hideout: A look inside Australia's most infamous shipping containerThe murder charges included special circumstances — lying in wait, committing crime for financial gain and murdering the witness in an investigation — that could carry the death penalty. Prosecutors haven't announced whether they will seek it.Attorneys for the Houston-born alt-pop singer said he was innocent in a statement released after homicide detectives arrested him on Thursday at a home in Hollywood."Let us be clear — the actual evidence in this case will show that David Burke did not murder Celeste Rivas Hernandez and he was not the cause of her death," the lawyers said.The long-dead body of Rivas Hernandez was found inside a Tesla that was towed from the Hollywood Hills on September 8, one day after she would have turned 15. Her family had reported her missing from her hometown of Lake Elsinore, about 110 kilometres south-east of Los Angeles.Authorities did not publicly name Burke as a suspect until his arrest. His lawyers' statement last week, in which said they "will vigorously defend David's innocence", was the first time they weighed in publicly.The singer had been under investigation by an LA County grand jury looking into the death. The probe was officially secret, but its existence — and his designation as its target — was revealed in February when his mother, father and brother objected in a Texas court to subpoenas demanding they testify.READ MORE: 'Mega-quake' warning, tsunami alert after tremorThe 2023 Tesla Model Y was registered in the singer's name at the Texas address of his subpoenaed family members, according to court filings from prosecutors. It had been towed from an upscale Hollywood Hills neighbourhood where it had been sitting as though it was abandoned.Police investigators searching the Tesla in a tow yard found a cadaver bag "covered with insects and a strong odour of decay", court documents said. Detectives partially unzipped a bag and found a head and torso.Investigators from the Los Angeles County Medical Examiner's Office removed the bag and "discovered the arms and legs had been severed from the body", according to court documents.A second black bag was found under the first, and dismembered body parts were inside it. No cause of death has been publicly revealed, and police got a judge to block the release details of the autopsy.READ MORE: Football legend found not guilty of sexually abusing girlOn Friday, the medical examiner told The Associated Press the court order remained in place after Burke's arrest, and suggested to ask the police whether they would ask to lift it.The family of Rivas Hernandez has remained private and has not made any public statements on her death or the case.D4vd, pronounced "David", gained popularity among Gen Z for his blend of indie rock, R&B and lo-fi pop. He went viral on TikTok in 2022 with the hit Romantic Homicide, which peaked at No.4 four on Billboard's Hot Rock & Alternative Songs chart. He then signed with Darkroom and Interscope Records and released his debut EP Petals to Thorns and a follow-up, The Lost Petals, in 2023.When the body was discovered, the singer continued his North American tour, but when reports of his possible involvement spread widely, he cancelled the final two shows and a European tour that was to follow.NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here

items.title: Gas tax fight escalates ahead of budget as inquiry begins

items.title: Japan warns of increased risk of mega-quake after 7.7-magnitude one
items.standfirst: An earthquake with a preliminary magnitude of 7.7 struck off the north-eastern coast of Japan, triggering tsunami warnings and widespread evacuation orders affecting around 170,000 people.
